{"type":"freeform","cover":{"titleText":"","subTitleText":"","presenterText":"","dateText":"","logos":[]},"section":{"sectionText":"","smallerSectionText":""},"freeform":{"content":[[{"type":"text","stretch":true,"fillArea":true,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"grey","placeholderInvisible":false,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"span":4,"markdown":"Since mid-2023, BCG vaccination volumes have averaged about 10 % below expected levels, reflecting a sustained but moderate shortfall in newborn immunization coverage that gradually stabilized within expected ranges by mid-2025.\n\nPenta 1 and Penta 3 remained within expected levels, with monthly differences ranging from about –3 % to +3 %."},{"type":"figure","stretch":true,"fillArea":false,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"none","placeholderInvisible":false,"useFigureAdditionalScale":true,"figureAdditionalScale":0.8,"presentationObjectInReportInfo":{"id":"cf3b04ed-e85d-4eda-bdc5-6575b0240821","moduleId":"m003","selectedReplicantValue":"","isDefault":false}}]],"useHeader":true,"headerText":"Slight disruptions for BCG, followed by recovery to expected levels in June 2025"}}

{"type":"freeform","cover":{"titleText":"","subTitleText":"","presenterText":"","dateText":"","logos":[]},"section":{"sectionText":"","smallerSectionText":""},"freeform":{"content":[[{"type":"text","span":2,"markdown":"Vitamin A supplementation volumes fluctuated between 350,000 and 500,000 from mid-2023 to mid-2025, with a 17 % rise in early 2024, a 10 % dip in Q3 2024, and a 13 % rebound in Q2 2025, likely reflecting routine campaign-driven variations.","stretch":true,"fillArea":true,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"grey","placeholderInvisible":false,"placeholderStretch":true,"placeholderHeight":50,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"imgHeight":300,"imgFit":"inside","imgStretch":true,"hideFigureCaption":false,"hideFigureSubCaption":false,"hideFigureFootnote":false},{"type":"figure","presentationObjectInReportInfo":{"id":"56984f6e-0c96-4f53-bfcf-1a111619906c","moduleId":"m003","isDefault":false,"selectedReplicantValue":""},"stretch":true,"fillArea":false,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"none","placeholderInvisible":false,"placeholderStretch":true,"placeholderHeight":50,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"imgHeight":300,"imgFit":"inside","imgStretch":true,"hideFigureCaption":false,"hideFigureSubCaption":false,"hideFigureFootnote":false},{"type":"figure","presentationObjectInReportInfo":{"id":"ecb1e308-7ddf-4cb3-b413-c1381feb4fd2","moduleId":"m003","isDefault":false,"selectedReplicantValue":""},"stretch":true,"fillArea":false,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"none","placeholderInvisible":false,"placeholderStretch":true,"placeholderHeight":50,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"imgHeight":300,"imgFit":"inside","imgStretch":true,"hideFigureCaption":false,"hideFigureSubCaption":false,"hideFigureFootnote":false}]],"useHeader":true,"headerText":"Trends in vitamin A supplementation in children (6-59 mo)","useFooter":false,"footerText":""}}

{"type":"freeform","cover":{"titleText":"","titleTextRelFontSize":10,"subTitleText":"","subTitleTextRelFontSize":6,"presenterText":"","presenterTextRelFontSize":4,"dateText":"","dateTextRelFontSize":3,"logos":[]},"section":{"sectionText":"","sectionTextRelFontSize":8,"smallerSectionText":"","smallerSectionTextRelFontSize":5},"freeform":{"content":[[{"type":"figure","stretch":true,"fillArea":false,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"none","placeholderInvisible":false,"placeholderStretch":true,"placeholderHeight":50,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"imgHeight":300,"imgFit":"inside","imgStretch":true,"hideFigureCaption":true,"hideFigureSubCaption":true,"hideFigureFootnote":false,"presentationObjectInReportInfo":{"id":"c772ef64-3b3d-4131-ad33-22dfc904d51f","moduleId":"m003","isDefault":false,"selectedReplicantValue":""}}],[{"type":"text","markdown":"The comparison of haemoglobin testing at ANC registration versus testing at ≥36 weeks shows a consistent gap in coverage, revealing weaknesses in sustained antenatal follow-up and late-pregnancy anaemia surveillance. While annual haemoglobin checks at registration remained high, ranging from about 914,000 women in 2020 to 970,582 in 2022, the number checked again near term was roughly 40–50% lower in every year of the review. For example, in 2022, 970,582 women were tested at registration but only 554,230 were tested again at 36 weeks — leaving over 416,000 pregnant women without late-stage haemoglobin monitoring that year. In 2025, both indicators show a steep decline: haemoglobin testing at registration dropped to 487,243 — almost half of the 2024 value (922,829) — while late-pregnancy testing dropped even further to 280,152. The scale of this reduction suggests a systemwide disruption affecting laboratory verification or follow-up contact, rather than reduced ANC attendance alone. This widening gap indicates that many women are being screened once during pregnancy but not monitored again when anaemia risk peaks, reducing the ability of the health system to detect and manage preventable maternal complications such as haemorrhage and low-birthweight risk.","stretch":true,"fillArea":false,"textSize":1,"textBackground":"primary","placeholderInvisible":false,"placeholderStretch":true,"placeholderHeight":50,"useFigureAdditionalScale":false,"figureAdditionalScale":1,"imgHeight":300,"imgFit":"inside","imgStretch":true,"hideFigureCaption":false,"hideFigureSubCaption":false,"hideFigureFootnote":false}]],"useHeader":true,"headerText":"Comparing Haemoglobin Checking at Registration and 36 weeks"}}
